macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: S&P 500 Hits Record Closing High as AI Optimism Offsets Middle East Tensions
items.summary: The S&P 500 reached a record closing high on Tuesday, while the Nasdaq gained further, driven by AI-fueled optimism in semiconductor stocks that outweighed concerns over escalating U.S.-Iran conflict.

items.title: Micron Technology Surges to $1 Trillion Market Value After UBS Price Target Hike
items.summary: Semiconductor stock Micron hit a $1 trillion market value for the first time after UBS raised its price target to $1,625 from $535, reflecting strong AI-driven demand.

items.title: IBM Shares Plunge Over 20% After Q2 Earnings Warning
items.summary: IBM stock skidded more than 20% midday after the company warned that second-quarter earnings fell short of market expectations.

items.title: U.S. Inflation Slows in June, Reducing Fed Rate Hike Expectations
items.summary: A surprise slowdown in U.S. inflation data for June curbed bets on a near-term Federal Reserve rate hike, leading to gains in Asian markets and a weaker U.S. dollar.

items.title: Dollar Weakens as Softer Inflation Data Dampens Fed Hike Bets Despite Oil Risks
items.summary: The U.S. dollar extended its weakness after tumbling from a two-week high, as softer-than-expected inflation reduced near-term rate hike expectations despite elevated oil prices fueling inflation risks.

items.title: China's Q2 Economic Growth Slows to 4.3%, Missing Expectations
items.summary: China's annual economic growth slowed sharply to 4.3% in the second quarter, its lowest level in over three years, missing the government's 2026 target due to weak domestic demand and the Iran oil shock.

items.title: Brent Crude Hits 1-Month High Near $85 Amid U.S.-Iran Fighting
items.summary: Oil prices hit a one-month high as renewed hostilities between Washington and Tehran clouded the outlook for the Strait of Hormuz, with Brent crude nearing $85 a barrel.

items.title: India-Britain Trade Pact Takes Effect, Cutting Tariffs on Thousands of Goods
items.summary: India and Britain's comprehensive trade pact became effective on Wednesday, cutting tariffs on thousands of goods and widening access for services firms and professionals in both markets.

items.title: ASML Beats Q2 Forecasts, Boosting European Tech Shares Despite Middle East Caution
items.summary: European shares were subdued as gains in technology stocks following strong forecasts from chip equipment maker ASML were overshadowed by caution over escalating Middle East tensions.

items.title: South Korea's KOSPI Enters Bear Market After 25% Fall from June Record
items.summary: South Korea's KOSPI has entered a bear market after falling about 25% from its record high reached in late June, highlighting a sharp reversal of one of the world's strongest equity rallies.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin and Ethereum Rally Past $64K and $1.8K as Cooler Inflation Dims Rate Hike Odds
items.summary: Leading cryptocurrencies jumped over 3–5% in 24 hours, with Bitcoin hitting $65,000 and Ethereum reclaiming $1,800, following softer-than-expected U.S. inflation data that reduced fears of rate hikes.

items.title: South Korea to Enforce New State Asset Management Law Covering Cryptocurrencies
items.summary: South Korea announced plans to bring crypto assets under a new state asset management law, signaling tighter regulatory oversight for digital assets in the country.

items.title: Ethereum Foundation Privacy Team Spins Out as For-Profit EthSystems with Lubin and Bitmine Backing
items.summary: The Ethereum Foundation’s privacy team has launched as a for-profit entity called EthSystems to serve institutional clients, backed by Vitalik Buterin collaborator Joseph Lubin and Bitmine.

items.title: Tether Freezes $131M USDT Linked to Iran Sanctions
items.summary: Tether froze four TRON wallets holding $131 million in USDT after U.S. sanctions linked them to Iran, aiming to block illicit crypto financing.

items.title: U.S. Government Transfers $288M in Seized Bitcoin and Ethereum to Coinbase Prime
items.summary: The U.S. government moved approximately $288 million worth of seized Bitcoin and Ethereum to Coinbase Prime, marking a significant step in asset liquidation or custody.

items.title: Binance Plans Crypto 'Super App' Expansion into Payments and Financial Services
items.summary: Binance is targeting a 'Super App' model by expanding beyond trading into payments and broader financial services, aligning with rising global stablecoin adoption.

items.title: Morgan Stanley Updates Ethereum and Solana ETF Filings with 0.14% Fee
items.summary: Morgan Stanley revised its Ethereum and Solana ETF filings to include a 0.14% management fee, with analysts expecting near-term product launches.

items.title: Coinbase Opens Registration for Mainland China Users with Local ID Cards
items.summary: Coinbase now allows mainland China users to register using Chinese identity cards and local addresses, significantly expanding access beyond prior restrictions.

items.title: Pi Network Plunges Over 16% in 24 Hours as Value Erodes
items.summary: Pi Network’s price fell from nearly $0.10 to $0.07 USDT, a drop of over 16%, reigniting criticism that the project resembles a failed speculative scheme.

items.title: Crypto Media Layoffs Continue: Decrypt, Blockworks, DL News Cut Staff
items.summary: Decrypt reportedly laid off several journalists, extending a wave of workforce reductions in crypto media that also hit Blockworks and DL News.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: OpenAI Releases GPT-5.6 Series with Three Configurations: Sol, Terra, and Luna
items.summary: OpenAI has moved its new GPT-5.6 series to general availability, featuring three configurations: the flagship Sol, balanced Terra, and low-cost high-speed Luna, with Microsoft researchers confirming Sol Ultra solved a 50-year-old math puzzle.

items.title: SpaceXAI Releases Grok 4.5 with 1.5 Trillion Parameters
items.summary: SpaceXAI (formerly xAI) released Grok 4.5 to the public, boasting a 1.5 trillion parameter count and pricing at $2 per million input tokens and $6 per million output tokens.

items.title: Meta Introduces Muse Spark 1.1 Multimodal Reasoning Model
items.summary: Meta introduced Muse Spark 1.1 from Superintelligence Labs, a multimodal reasoning model highlighting significant improvements in agent behavior and coding capabilities.

items.title: SambaNova Systems Raises $1B, Reaching $11B Valuation
items.summary: AI semiconductor company SambaNova Systems raised $1 billion in the first close of its Series F, achieving a post-money valuation of $11 billion.

items.title: New York Imposes One-Year Moratorium on Large Data Center Construction
items.summary: New York became the first U.S. state to halt construction of large new data centers for one year due to concerns over rising power costs, water strain, and community burden.

items.title: Anthropic's Claude Restrictions Lifted by Trump Administration
items.summary: The Trump administration lifted a weekslong ban on Anthropic's latest Claude chatbot versions, ending restrictions tied to cybersecurity concerns.

items.title: Meta Pulls AI Feature After Instagram Account Criticism
items.summary: Meta disabled a feature of a recently launched AI tool following criticism that it used Instagram accounts to create AI-generated images without proper consent.

items.title: Reflection Secures $1B+ Computing Deal with Nebius for Nvidia Chips
items.summary: AI startup Reflection signed a deal exceeding $1 billion to secure computing capacity from Nebius, including access to Nvidia's latest AI chips.

items.title: Fujitsu Launches Kozuchi Multi AI Agent Framework for Self-Evolving Agents
items.summary: Fujitsu announced the 'Kozuchi Multi AI Agent Framework,' a platform for self-evolving multi-AI agents that autonomously optimize entire business processes.

items.title: Hugging Face Transformers Now Run at Native Speeds in Veilm Inference Engine
items.summary: Hugging Face announced that its Transformers library models can now run at native speeds within the Veilm inference engine, eliminating a major deployment pain point for developers.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: GTA VI Price Debate Intensifies as Analyst Predicts $200 Release Price
items.summary: An analyst's claim that GTA VI could be sold for up to $200 has reignited industry debates over premium game pricing, deluxe editions, and monetization strategies.

items.title: Palworld Surpasses 850,000 Players on Steam Following Return
items.summary: Palworld has seen a massive resurgence, exceeding 850,000 concurrent players on Steam as the game returns to prominence.

items.title: Call of Duty: Black Ops 1 and 2 Drop on PlayStation with Major Launch Discounts
items.summary: The original Call of Duty: Black Ops 1 and 2 have been released on PlayStation platforms, featuring significant launch discounts that have driven strong sales.

items.title: Ubisoft Surprisingly Expands The Division Resurgence to PC in Early Access
items.summary: Ubisoft has unexpectedly expanded The Division Resurgence from a mobile-only title to PC, with the game now available in early access via Ubisoft's store.

items.title: Epic Games Store Offers Free Horror Game Together After Dark Until July 27
items.summary: The Epic Games Store has launched a limited-time giveaway for the horror game Together After Dark, available for free claim until July 27, 2026.

items.title: Battlefield 6 Season 4 Introduces Naval Combat Features
items.summary: Battlefield 6's Season 4 has been revealed, featuring new naval combat mechanics alongside the massive Game Update 1.3.3.0.

items.title: Dying Light: The Beast PS4 and Xbox One Versions Officially Cancelled
items.summary: The PS4 and Xbox One versions of Dying Light: The Beast have been cancelled, limiting the game's release to newer console generations.

items.title: Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced Receives PC Cinematics Patch
items.summary: A new patch for Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced has been released to fix cinematic issues on PC.

items.title: Agent 64: Spies Never Die Announced for Steam Release on August 11
items.summary: The shooter Agent 64: Spies Never Die will launch on Steam on August 11, featuring a 14-mission campaign and multiplayer for up to eight players.

items.title: Agefield High: Rock the School Set for PC Launch on August 12
items.summary: Agefield High: Rock the School, an open-world school adventure set in the early 2000s, will launch on PC via Steam on August 12.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Stripe and Advent International Propose $53 Billion Acquisition of PayPal
items.summary: Payments company Stripe and private equity firm Advent International have made a joint offer to acquire PayPal Holdings Inc at $60.50 per share, valuing the company at over $53 billion.

items.title: ASML Raises 2026 Financial Forecasts and Plans Capacity Expansion
items.summary: ASML, the world's largest supplier of chip manufacturing equipment, increased its 2026 financial forecasts and announced capacity expansion following better-than-expected Q2 earnings, though the report notes this is driven by AI demand.

items.title: Boston Dynamics Envisions 'Spot' Robot for Human Delivery Assistance
items.summary: Boston Dynamics is in talks with major logistics companies to deploy its four-legged 'Spot' robot to assist human delivery workers.

items.title: Google Images Celebrates 25th Anniversary with New Features
items.summary: Google Images marked its 25th birthday by adding a personalized gallery feature allowing users to save web images in Pinterest-like collections, alongside other updates.

items.title: Telegram's t.me Domain Suspended Amid US Treasury Crackdown
items.summary: The t.me domain for Telegram was suspended as a top-level domain provider complied with a US Treasury crackdown on a VPN service linked to ransomware groups.

items.title: New York Blocks New Large Data Centers for Up to One Year
items.summary: New York state will block the construction of new large data centers that fuel AI for up to a year to protect the environment and energy grid.

items.title: IBM Sparks Market Rout with Below-Estimate Revenue Forecast
items.summary: IBM triggered a market downturn after forecasting second-quarter revenue below estimates, signaling that businesses are prioritizing data-center infrastructure spending over software.

items.title: NASA Astronaut and Russian Cosmonauts Launch to International Space Station
items.summary: NASA astronaut Anil Menon and two Russian cosmonauts successfully launched to the ISS via the Soyuz MS-29 spacecraft from Kazakhstan for an eight-month mission.

items.title: India's HEX20 Successfully Launches Second Satellite KOYO
items.summary: Thiruvananthapuram-based HEX20 launched its second satellite, KOYO, for Taiwan's National Central University, confirming operational status in space with six more missions scheduled.

items.title: India Tests Pinaka Long Range Guided Rocket with Precision
items.summary: India successfully conducted a flight test of its Pinaka Long Range Guided Rocket at Chandipur, demonstrating maneuvers and precise target impact at a minimum range of 60 kilometers.
